title: GROWTH-DEPTH-2
scope: Pivots off the inbox/legal-hold surface concentration of cycles 62-65 (4 of last 5 cycles touched messaging/compliance). Reopens the Phase 56 GROWTH-ATTRIB-1 lineage with four product-led-growth surfaces: (1) in-app NPS surveys with server-authoritative cadence gating + global kill-switch + Inertia-shared prompt + NPS score rollup; (2) referral leaderboard ranking top referrers on the Phase-34 referrals ledger with DPA-grade anonymisation + per-landlord opt-out; (3) a WORLD-CLASS standalone in-app onboarding tour subsystem (spotlight overlay + @floating-ui/vue viewport-aware tooltip positioning + cross-route orchestration + milestone-aware branching off Phase-46 OnboardingMilestone + server-authoritative resume + full a11y/RTL/i18n/mobile) — built additively so Phases 37-65 surface stays stable; (4) cohort retention deepening that partitions Phase-57 ChurnService::cohortsBySource into a referral-vs-organic comparison with delta-vs-baseline + significance gating, ops-only. Scout (2026-05-20) confirmed: referrals table (Phase-34) + Referral model + ReferralAttributionService::attribute live; users.acquisition_source enum (organic/referral/paid/invitation/unknown) backfilled Phase-56; ChurnService::cohortsBySource (Phase-57) already pivots retention by acquisition_source on read-replica — Phase 66 extends it, does not reinvent; ProductEvent (TenantScope, append-only) is the event ledger; NotificationPreference + users.last_active_at + QuietHoursService are the eligibility-gating primitives; OnboardingMilestone FUNNEL const (SIGNED_UP/FIRST_PROPERTY/FIRST_UNIT/FIRST_TENANT/FIRST_INVOICE/FIRST_PAYMENT) + OnboardingMilestoneRecorder are the tour-branching signals; HandleInertiaRequests::share is the prompt/tour delivery channel (auth.* + Inertia::defer pattern); Modal.vue + SlideOutPanel.vue + useFocusTrap/useEscapeKey/useAnnouncer/useBodyScrollLock are the a11y composable kit; useI18n + lang/{en,sw,ar} + ESLint no-hardcoded-english-strings is the i18n contract (ar=TODO-prefix per Phase-43); MetricsService::gauge + routes/console.php $schedule wrapper (withoutOverlapping/onOneServer/Africa-Nairobi) + alert-thresholds.md table is the observability contract; NO tour/shepherd/popper dep exists today (Phase 66 adds @floating-ui/vue as the single focused new frontend dependency, by explicit user authorisation for world-class output). Six categories x 3 = 18 findings.

categories.NPS-SURVEY: In-app Net Promoter Score surveys with server-authoritative gating so the prompt cannot be spammed or faked from the client. (1) NPS-SURVEY-1 [HIGH] — nps_responses table (landlord_id TenantScope FK, user_id respondent FK, score unsignedTinyInteger 0-10, category enum detractor|passive|promoter derived, comment text nullable <=1000, context string nullable, prompted_at, responded_at, softDeletes, timestamps; index [landlord_id, created_at] + [user_id, created_at]) + NpsResponse model (TenantScope + SoftDeletes; CATEGORY_THRESHOLDS const 0-6 detractor / 7-8 passive / 9-10 promoter; categorise(int): string static). App\Http\Controllers\NpsResponseController::store validates score 0-10 + comment + context allow-list, derives category, double-submit idempotent (rejects a second response inside the active cadence window via NpsEligibilityService::hasRespondedRecently — returns 422 not a duplicate row), records responded_at on nps_prompt_states. NpsResponsePolicy::create gates respondent owns the prompt. (2) NPS-SURVEY-2 [HIGH] — server-authoritative eligibility: nps_prompt_states table (user_id unique, last_prompted_at, last_responded_at, dismiss_count unsignedSmallInteger, opted_out_at nullable, snoozed_until nullable, timestamps). App\Services\Growth\NpsEligibilityService::shouldPrompt(User): bool gates on config('nps.enabled') GLOBAL KILL-SWITCH first, then account age >= config('nps.min_account_age_days', 14), no response within config('nps.cadence_days', 90), dismiss_count < config('nps.max_dismissals', 3) AND snoozed_until passed, not opted_out, not in QuietHoursService quiet hours. promptPayloadFor(User): ?array for Inertia. The prompt IMPRESSION is recorded server-side (POST /nps/impression -> markPrompted) so a client re-render or refresh cannot re-trigger and a tampered client cannot suppress the cadence — the server is the single source of truth. config/nps.php new (enabled/cadence_days/snooze_days/max_dismissals/min_account_age_days/contexts allow-list). HandleInertiaRequests shares auth.nps_prompt via Inertia::defer (null when ineligible). (3) NPS-SURVEY-3 [LOW] — Components/Nps/NpsSurveyModal.vue: branded 0-10 segmented scale (color-graded detractor->promoter), optional comment textarea revealed after score select, Submit + 'Not now' (POST /nps/dismiss -> dismiss_count++ + snoozed_until) + 'Don't ask again' (POST /nps/opt-out). Mounted once in AuthenticatedLayout gated by auth.nps_prompt; on mount POSTs impression. Reuses useFocusTrap/useEscapeKey/useAnnouncer. lang/en/nps.php + en.json nps.* keys (sw full, ar TODO-prefix). Honors prefers-reduced-motion + RTL.
categories.REFERRAL-LEADERBOARD: Top-referrer leaderboard on the Phase-34 referrals ledger with privacy-by-default. (1) REFERRAL-LEADERBOARD-1 [HIGH] — App\Services\Growth\ReferralLeaderboardService::topReferrers(int $limit, bool $anonymise, ?int $viewerId): array groups referrals by referrer_user_id, composite score = attributed_count + (rewarded_count * config('referral.leaderboard.reward_weight', 2)), ranks desc, slices top-N (cap config('referral.leaderboard.max', 50)). PRIVACY HIGH: opted-out referrers (users.leaderboard_opt_out=true) are EXCLUDED from the result set entirely (DPA right not to be displayed); when $anonymise=true the service NEVER returns name/email in the payload — only rank + a masked handle (e.g. 'Top Referrer #4') computed server-side, so PII cannot leak via client inspection. The viewer's OWN row is always returned de-anonymised + flagged is_self even if outside top-N (viewer always sees their real rank). Cache::remember 600s keyed by (limit, anonymise). (2) REFERRAL-LEADERBOARD-2 [MEDIUM] — two HTTP surfaces: GET /growth/leaderboard (auth landlord, ALWAYS anonymise=true except is_self row, returns top-N + caller position) via App\Http\Controllers\Growth\ReferralLeaderboardController::index; GET /ops/growth/referral-leaderboard (super_admin only, anonymise=false full names for ops) via OpsReferralLeaderboardController::index. Both Inertia::render. Landlord route under auth+verified; ops route under role:super_admin. (3) REFERRAL-LEADERBOARD-3 [MEDIUM] — users.leaderboard_opt_out boolean default false migration + POST /growth/leaderboard/opt-out toggle (App\Http\Controllers\Growth\LeaderboardOptOutController::__invoke) writing the column + busting the leaderboard cache. Pages/Growth/Leaderboard.vue branded ranked list (medal styling top-3, own-row highlight + 'You are #N', masked handles for others), opt-out toggle with DPA explainer copy. lang/en/growth.php leaderboard.* keys (sw full, ar TODO).
categories.ONBOARDING-TOUR: WORLD-CLASS standalone in-app onboarding tour subsystem (user-authorised additive build; @floating-ui/vue single new dep). (1) ONBOARDING-TOUR-1 [HIGH] — engine + server-authoritative state. user_tour_states table (id, user_id FK, tour_key string, current_step unsignedSmallInteger default 0, status enum active|completed|dismissed default active, started_at, last_advanced_at, completed_at nullable, dismissed_at nullable, timestamps; unique [user_id, tour_key]). App\Models\UserTourState. App\Services\Onboarding\TourService: registry of tour definitions (per-role step lists), stepsFor(User, string $tourKey): array filters steps by UNREACHED Phase-46 OnboardingMilestone (a landlord who already added a property never sees the 'add your first property' step — milestone-aware branching), stateFor(User, $tourKey) returns/creates UserTourState, payloadFor(User) builds the Inertia auth.onboarding_tour bundle (active tour_key + filtered steps + resume current_step) returning null when completed|dismissed (NO re-trigger correctness — a completed/dismissed tour is terminal server-side). resources/js/composables/useOnboardingTour.ts orchestrates: target resolution via [data-tour] selectors, @floating-ui/vue computePosition (autoUpdate on scroll/resize, flip+shift+offset middleware, RTL placement), cross-route advance (router.visit to a step's route then resume), spotlight rect tracking, persistence POSTs (debounced advance). (2) ONBOARDING-TOUR-2 [MEDIUM] — world-class UI components + persistence endpoints. Components/Tour/TourOverlay.vue (full-viewport dimmed backdrop with an animated spotlight cutout via SVG mask around the active target, prefers-reduced-motion aware) + Components/Tour/TourTooltip.vue (@floating-ui-positioned card: title + body + 'Step X of Y' progress dots + Back/Next/Skip, brand-styled, mobile-responsive bottom-sheet fallback < sm). Full a11y: useFocusTrap on the tooltip, useEscapeKey to dismiss, useAnnouncer aria-live step announcements, keyboard nav (Arrow/Enter/Esc), role=dialog aria-modal, RTL-aware arrow inversion (Phase-44 useRtlAware). App\Http\Controllers\Onboarding\OnboardingTourController: POST /onboarding-tour/advance {tour_key, step}, POST /onboarding-tour/complete {tour_key}, POST /onboarding-tour/dismiss {tour_key} — all server-authoritative on UserTourState; advance rejects backward writes past completed. [data-tour] anchors added to AuthenticatedLayout nav + Dashboard key cards (additive attributes only, no structural refactor). (3) ONBOARDING-TOUR-3 [LOW] — role-specific tour definitions (landlord dashboard tour ~6 steps, caretaker ~3, tenant ~3) in TourService registry keyed to milestones; lang/en/onboarding.php tour.* copy (sw full, ar TODO-prefix); TourOverlay mounted once in AuthenticatedLayout gated by auth.onboarding_tour.active with a first-eligible-login auto-start (only when UserTourState absent); package.json gains @floating-ui/vue. Tour registry shaped so future tours (e.g. reports tour) drop in without engine changes.
categories.COHORT-RETENTION: Deepen Phase-57 cohortsBySource into an actionable referral-vs-organic comparison, ops-only. (1) COHORT-RETENTION-1 [HIGH] — App\Services\Growth\CohortRetentionService::sourceComparison(int $monthsBack = 12): array builds per-acquisition_source retention curves and a delta-vs-organic baseline per month-offset. SECURITY HIGH: the cross-tenant aggregate reads users withoutGlobalScopes ONLY behind an explicit super-admin guard inside the service (assertSuperAdmin or accepts a pre-authorised flag from the controller) — a landlord-scoped caller must NEVER reach the unscoped path; the landlord-facing variant scopes to that landlord's own referred users. Significance gating: a source row is flagged insufficient_sample when cohort size < config('growth.cohort.min_sample', 20) so ops doesn't over-read noise. Reuses ChurnService::cohortsBySource as the per-source data source, layering the comparison/delta on top (no duplicate cohort SQL). (2) COHORT-RETENTION-2 [MEDIUM] — App\Http\Controllers\Ops\OpsCohortRetentionController::index (role:super_admin) Inertia::render('Ops/Growth/CohortRetention') with source_comparison + baseline + month range props; GET /ops/growth/cohort-retention route. Surfaces retention[] curves, delta badges, insufficient_sample flags. (3) COHORT-RETENTION-3 [LOW] — Pages/Ops/Growth/CohortRetention.vue retention table/heatmap (rows = source, columns = month offset, cell = retention %, color-scaled), delta-vs-organic column, insufficient-sample muting, AuthenticatedLayout. lang/en/growth.php cohort.* keys.
categories.GROWTH-OBSERVABILITY: Telemetry + ops surfacing for the three new growth surfaces. (1) GROWTH-OBSERVABILITY-1 [MEDIUM] — App\Services\Growth\NpsScoreService::compute(?int $landlordId, int $windowDays = 90): array returns NPS = round(%promoters - %detractors) + response_count + response_rate + breakdown. App\Console\Commands\NpsRollup ('nps:rollup') daily 04:50 Africa/Nairobi emits nps_score{scope} + nps_response_count{scope} + nps_response_rate{scope} gauges (platform scope + per-active-landlord). Registered in routes/console.php via the $schedule wrapper. (2) GROWTH-OBSERVABILITY-2 [MEDIUM] — App\Console\Commands\GrowthLeaderboardRollup ('growth:leaderboard-rollup') daily 04:55 emits referral_leaderboard_participants + referral_leaderboard_top_score gauges; onboarding tour gauges onboarding_tour_completed_count + onboarding_tour_dismissed_count + onboarding_tour_active_count emitted from a tour rollup (or folded into the same command) — all via MetricsService::gauge. Tour completion/dismiss also increment a counter at the controller for real-time signal. (3) GROWTH-OBSERVABILITY-3 [MEDIUM] — docs/runbooks/alert-thresholds.md gains rows for nps_score (sev4 visibility, paging only on sustained <0 over 7d), onboarding_tour_dismissed_count (sev4 — dismiss spike => tour copy/targeting problem), referral_leaderboard_participants (visibility); OpsGrowthAttribution dashboard gains an NPS score card + cohort-retention link + leaderboard link (additive cards on the existing Ops/Growth/Attribution.vue), wiring the four surfaces into one growth pane.
categories.CI: (1) CI-1 [MEDIUM] — tests/Feature/Growth/Phase66GrowthDepth2SurfaceTest cross-category presence map: nps_responses + nps_prompt_states + user_tour_states tables exist with key columns; users.leaderboard_opt_out present; NpsResponse/NpsResponseController/NpsResponsePolicy/NpsEligibilityService + ReferralLeaderboardService/ReferralLeaderboardController/OpsReferralLeaderboardController/LeaderboardOptOutController + UserTourState/TourService/OnboardingTourController + CohortRetentionService/OpsCohortRetentionController + NpsScoreService classes exist; routes registered (nps.store/impression/dismiss/opt-out, growth.leaderboard + opt-out, ops referral-leaderboard, onboarding-tour.advance/complete/dismiss, ops cohort-retention); Vue files exist (NpsSurveyModal, Growth/Leaderboard, Tour/TourOverlay + TourTooltip, Ops/Growth/CohortRetention); composables/useOnboardingTour.ts present; @floating-ui/vue in package.json; config/nps.php loads; nps:rollup + growth:leaderboard-rollup commands registered. (2) CI-2 [MEDIUM] — per-category feature tests: Phase66NpsSurveyTest (score 0-10 validation + category derivation boundaries 6/7/8/9 + double-submit 422 inside cadence + kill-switch off => never eligible + account-age gate + dismiss increments + opt-out terminal + impression records server-side); Phase66ReferralLeaderboardTest (ranking by composite score + anonymise strips name/email from payload + opted-out excluded + viewer self-row de-anonymised even outside top-N + landlord route forces anonymise + ops route allows names + cross-landlord isolation); Phase66OnboardingTourTest (advance/complete/dismiss server-authoritative + completed tour returns null payload no re-trigger + dismissed terminal + milestone-aware step filtering skips reached milestones + backward-advance past completed rejected + cross-user state isolation); Phase66CohortRetentionService Test (super-admin unscoped path reachable only via guard + landlord-scoped variant excludes other landlords + delta-vs-organic math + insufficient_sample flag under min_sample). (3) CI-3 [LOW] — docs/runbooks/growth.md extended with NPS + leaderboard + onboarding-tour + cohort-retention operator sections (cadence config, kill-switch, opt-out/DPA, tour registry authoring, cron interaction matrix); alert-thresholds rows cross-linked; memory phase66 plan + pending-path + forward-path + MEMORY.md updated; CodeRabbit code-reviewer agent run recorded per sub-phase.

findings.title: nps_responses table + NpsResponse model + idempotent store controller
findings.evidence: There is no NPS/feedback survey surface today (only legacy per-ticket TicketFeedback, no multi-tenancy). Product-led growth needs a structured NPS signal. A naive store endpoint would (a) accept out-of-range scores, (b) write duplicate rows on double-click / refresh, and (c) lack tenant isolation — letting a respondent's row leak across landlords.
findings.remediation: Migration 2026_05_20_660100_phase66_nps1_create_nps_responses: landlord_id FK->users cascadeOnDelete (TenantScope ownership), user_id FK->users, score unsignedTinyInteger, category enum(detractor,passive,promoter), comment text nullable, context string nullable, prompted_at + responded_at timestamps, softDeletes, timestamps; index [landlord_id, created_at] + [user_id, created_at]. App\Models\NpsResponse uses TenantScope + SoftDeletes; const CATEGORY_THRESHOLDS; static categorise(int $score): string (0-6 detractor, 7-8 passive, 9-10 promoter). App\Http\Controllers\NpsResponseController::store validates score integer 0-10, comment <=1000, context in config('nps.contexts'); NpsResponsePolicy::create; rejects 2nd response within cadence window (NpsEligibilityService::hasRespondedRecently) with 422; on success derives category, persists row, calls NpsEligibilityService::markResponded. Phase66NpsSurveyTest asserts boundary categories + double-submit 422 + tenant isolation.

findings.title: Server-authoritative eligibility + global kill-switch + impression recording
findings.evidence: If eligibility/cadence lives client-side, a tampered or buggy client can spam the survey (fatigue, skewed data) or suppress it (no data). There must be a server-side single source of truth for whether to prompt, plus a global kill-switch to stop all prompts instantly if NPS data is found to be harmful or during incidents.
findings.remediation: Migration 2026_05_20_660110_phase66_nps2_create_nps_prompt_states: user_id unique FK, last_prompted_at, last_responded_at, dismiss_count unsignedSmallInteger default 0, opted_out_at nullable, snoozed_until nullable, timestamps. config/nps.php: enabled (kill-switch), cadence_days 90, snooze_days 30, max_dismissals 3, min_account_age_days 14, contexts allow-list. App\Services\Growth\NpsEligibilityService::shouldPrompt evaluates kill-switch FIRST, then account age, cadence (no response in cadence_days), dismiss/ snooze limits, opt-out, QuietHoursService. promptPayloadFor(User): ?array. markPrompted/markResponded/markDismissed/optOut mutate nps_prompt_states. POST /nps/impression -> markPrompted (server records the impression so refresh/re-render cannot re-trigger and a client cannot fake suppression). HandleInertiaRequests shares auth.nps_prompt via Inertia::defer (null when ineligible). Phase66NpsSurveyTest asserts kill-switch off => never eligible, age gate, cadence gate, impression recorded.

findings.title: NpsSurveyModal.vue mounted globally + dismiss/opt-out + i18n + a11y
findings.evidence: Eligibility + storage are useless without a respectful, accessible prompt UI. The modal must offer respond / not-now / never, must not trap users, and must honor reduced-motion + RTL (Phase-44 contract).
findings.remediation: Components/Nps/NpsSurveyModal.vue: branded 0-10 segmented scale (color-graded), comment textarea revealed after score select, Submit (POST /nps), 'Not now' (POST /nps/dismiss), 'Don't ask again' (POST /nps/opt-out). Mounted once in AuthenticatedLayout gated by auth.nps_prompt; POSTs /nps/impression on mount. Reuses useFocusTrap + useEscapeKey + useAnnouncer; prefers-reduced-motion + RTL aware. lang/en/nps.php + en.json nps.* (sw full, ar TODO-prefix). Phase66GrowthDepth2SurfaceTest asserts file + i18n key presence.

findings.title: ReferralLeaderboardService with DPA anonymisation + opt-out exclusion
findings.evidence: A leaderboard over the referrals ledger surfaces other landlords' identities — a privacy hazard. Anonymisation that only hides names in the Vue layer still ships PII in the JSON payload (trivially inspected). Opted-out users must not appear at all. The viewer must still see their own real rank.
findings.remediation: App\Services\Growth\ReferralLeaderboardService::topReferrers(int $limit, bool $anonymise, ?int $viewerId): array groups referrals by referrer_user_id, composite score = attributed + rewarded*config('referral.leaderboard.reward_weight',2), ranks desc, slices min($limit, config('referral.leaderboard.max',50)). Excludes users.leaderboard_opt_out=true entirely. When $anonymise=true the payload contains ONLY rank + masked handle + score (no name/email built at all — PII never leaves the server). Viewer's own row always de-anonymised + is_self=true even if outside top-N. Cache::remember 600s keyed (limit, anonymise). Phase66ReferralLeaderboardTest asserts payload has no name/email when anonymised, opted-out excluded, self-row de-anonymised.

findings.title: Landlord (anonymised) + ops (full) leaderboard HTTP surfaces
findings.evidence: The service needs two distinct trust boundaries: landlords see an anonymised board + their own position; ops/super-admin see full names for support. Collapsing them risks leaking names to landlords.
findings.remediation: App\Http\Controllers\Growth\ReferralLeaderboardController::index (auth+verified) calls topReferrers(limit, anonymise:true, viewerId:auth id) -> Inertia 'Growth/Leaderboard'. App\Http\Controllers\Ops\OpsReferralLeaderboardController::index (role:super_admin) calls topReferrers(limit, anonymise:false) -> Inertia 'Ops/Growth/ReferralLeaderboard'. Routes: GET /growth/leaderboard (name growth.leaderboard), GET /ops/growth/referral-leaderboard. Phase66ReferralLeaderboardTest asserts landlord route forces anonymise + ops route allows names + non-super-admin blocked from ops route.

findings.title: leaderboard_opt_out column + toggle endpoint + Leaderboard.vue
findings.evidence: DPA / right-not-to-be-displayed requires a real opt-out the landlord controls, persisted server-side, that immediately removes them from the board (cache must bust).
findings.remediation: Migration 2026_05_20_660200_phase66_leaderboard1_add_opt_out_to_users (leaderboard_opt_out boolean default false). POST /growth/leaderboard/opt-out via App\Http\Controllers\Growth\LeaderboardOptOutController::__invoke writes the column + busts the leaderboard cache. Pages/Growth/Leaderboard.vue: medal-styled top-3, own-row highlight 'You are #N', masked handles for others, opt-out toggle + DPA explainer. lang/en/growth.php leaderboard.* (sw full, ar TODO). Phase66ReferralLeaderboardTest asserts opt-out excludes from subsequent board + cache bust.

findings.title: Tour engine: server-authoritative state + milestone-aware steps + @floating-ui orchestration
findings.evidence: A world-class tour must resume exactly where the user left off, never re-trigger after completion/dismissal, and never show steps for milestones already reached (showing 'add your first property' to a landlord with 10 properties is the hallmark of a cheap tour). Client-only state loses all of this on cache clear and is tamperable. Tooltip positioning hand-rolled with getBoundingClientRect breaks on scroll/resize/RTL/viewport-edge.
findings.remediation: Migration 2026_05_20_660300_phase66_tour1_create_user_tour_states: id, user_id FK, tour_key string, current_step unsignedSmallInteger default 0, status enum(active,completed,dismissed) default active, started_at, last_advanced_at, completed_at nullable, dismissed_at nullable, timestamps; unique [user_id, tour_key]. App\Models\UserTourState. App\Services\Onboarding\TourService: tour-definition registry (per-role step lists with milestone gates + optional route per step), stepsFor(User,$tourKey) filters out steps whose gating OnboardingMilestone is already reached, stateFor/payloadFor returns null when status in (completed,dismissed) (terminal — no re-trigger). resources/js/composables/useOnboardingTour.ts: [data-tour] target resolution, @floating-ui/vue computePosition + autoUpdate (flip/shift/offset, RTL placement), cross-route advance (router.visit then resume), spotlight rect tracking, debounced persistence POSTs. Phase66OnboardingTourTest asserts terminal-state null payload + milestone-aware filtering + isolation.

findings.title: TourOverlay + TourTooltip world-class components + persistence endpoints
findings.evidence: The visible tour must be polished and fully accessible: animated spotlight, viewport-aware tooltip, progress, keyboard nav, focus management, aria-live announcements, RTL, mobile bottom-sheet. Persistence must be server-authoritative and reject illegal transitions (advancing a completed tour).
findings.remediation: Components/Tour/TourOverlay.vue (dimmed backdrop + SVG-mask animated spotlight cutout around active [data-tour] target, prefers-reduced-motion aware) + Components/Tour/TourTooltip.vue (@floating-ui-positioned card: title, body, 'Step X of Y' dots, Back/Next/Skip; mobile <sm bottom-sheet). a11y: useFocusTrap on tooltip, useEscapeKey dismiss, useAnnouncer step announce, Arrow/Enter/Esc keys, role=dialog aria-modal, Phase-44 useRtlAware arrow inversion. App\Http\Controllers\Onboarding\OnboardingTourController: POST /onboarding-tour/advance|complete|dismiss, server-authoritative on UserTourState (advance rejects writes once completed). [data-tour] anchors added additively to AuthenticatedLayout nav + Dashboard cards (attributes only). Phase66OnboardingTourTest asserts advance/complete/dismiss transitions + backward-past-completed rejected.

findings.title: Role tour definitions + i18n copy + global mount + @floating-ui dep
findings.evidence: Engine + components need concrete per-role content, translations, and a single mount that auto-starts for first-eligible users only. The new dependency must be declared.
findings.remediation: TourService registry: landlord dashboard tour (~6 steps milestone-gated), caretaker (~3), tenant (~3). lang/en/onboarding.php tour.* copy (sw full, ar TODO-prefix). TourOverlay mounted once in AuthenticatedLayout gated by auth.onboarding_tour.active, auto-start only when UserTourState absent for the tour_key. package.json adds @floating-ui/vue. Registry shaped so a future tour drops in without engine edits. Phase66GrowthDepth2SurfaceTest asserts dep + mount + registry presence.

findings.title: CohortRetentionService::sourceComparison with super-admin-gated unscoped read
findings.evidence: Cross-tenant cohort aggregates require reading users withoutGlobalScopes — a serious data-exposure path if a landlord-scoped caller can reach it. Without sample-size gating, ops over-reads noise from tiny cohorts.
findings.remediation: App\Services\Growth\CohortRetentionService::sourceComparison(int $monthsBack=12): array layers a per-source comparison + delta-vs-organic baseline (per month-offset) on top of ChurnService::cohortsBySource (no duplicate cohort SQL). The unscoped (withoutGlobalScopes) aggregate is reachable ONLY behind an explicit super-admin guard inside the service; a landlord-scoped variant restricts to the landlord's own referred users. Rows with cohort size < config('growth.cohort.min_sample',20) flagged insufficient_sample. Phase66CohortRetentionServiceTest asserts guard blocks non-super-admin unscoped path + landlord variant excludes other landlords + delta math + insufficient_sample flag.

findings.title: Ops cohort-retention controller + route
findings.evidence: The comparison needs an ops-only surface; exposing it to landlords would leak cross-tenant retention.
findings.remediation: App\Http\Controllers\Ops\OpsCohortRetentionController::index (role:super_admin) -> Inertia::render('Ops/Growth/CohortRetention', [source_comparison, baseline, month_range]). GET /ops/growth/cohort-retention route under super_admin middleware. Phase66GrowthDepth2SurfaceTest asserts route + controller; Phase66CohortRetentionServiceTest covers the service auth.

findings.title: Pages/Ops/Growth/CohortRetention.vue retention heatmap
findings.evidence: Ops needs to read the comparison at a glance: source rows, month-offset columns, retention %, delta-vs-organic, insufficient-sample muting.
findings.remediation: Pages/Ops/Growth/CohortRetention.vue: table/heatmap (rows=source, cols=month offset, cell=retention% color-scaled), delta-vs-organic column with up/down badges, insufficient-sample rows muted, AuthenticatedLayout. lang/en/growth.php cohort.* keys. Phase66GrowthDepth2SurfaceTest asserts file presence.

findings.title: NpsScoreService + nps:rollup cron gauges
findings.evidence: NPS is only actionable as a rolled-up score (%promoters - %detractors) trended over time per scope; without a rollup cron there is no ops signal and no alerting substrate.
findings.remediation: App\Services\Growth\NpsScoreService::compute(?int $landlordId, int $windowDays=90): array returns nps score + response_count + response_rate + breakdown. App\Console\Commands\NpsRollup ('nps:rollup') daily 04:50 Africa/Nairobi via routes/console.php $schedule wrapper emits nps_score{scope} + nps_response_count{scope} + nps_response_rate{scope} (platform + per-active-landlord) through MetricsService::gauge. Phase66GrowthDepth2SurfaceTest asserts command registered; a focused test asserts NPS math.

findings.title: Referral + onboarding-tour gauges
findings.evidence: Leaderboard health and tour funnel health are invisible without gauges; tour dismiss-rate is a key product signal (high dismiss => copy/targeting problem).
findings.remediation: App\Console\Commands\GrowthLeaderboardRollup ('growth:leaderboard-rollup') daily 04:55 emits referral_leaderboard_participants + referral_leaderboard_top_score; tour gauges onboarding_tour_active_count + onboarding_tour_completed_count + onboarding_tour_dismissed_count emitted from the same rollup (counts over user_tour_states). OnboardingTourController also bumps a real-time counter on complete/dismiss. All via MetricsService. Phase66GrowthDepth2SurfaceTest asserts command registered.

findings.title: alert-thresholds rows + Ops growth dashboard surfacing
findings.evidence: New gauges need documented thresholds + on-call hints, and the four surfaces should be reachable from one ops growth pane instead of scattered URLs.
findings.remediation: docs/runbooks/alert-thresholds.md rows: nps_score (sev4, page only on sustained <0 over 7d), onboarding_tour_dismissed_count (sev4, dismiss spike => copy/targeting), referral_leaderboard_participants (visibility). Ops/Growth/Attribution.vue gains an NPS score card + links to cohort-retention + referral-leaderboard (additive). Phase66GrowthDepth2SurfaceTest asserts dashboard tokens; runbook checked in CI-3.

findings.title: Phase66GrowthDepth2SurfaceTest cross-category presence map
findings.evidence: PHPUnit cannot compile Vue; a surface watchdog catches refactor drift (deleted/renamed controllers, routes, services, Vue files, config, deps) across all six categories.
findings.remediation: tests/Feature/Growth/Phase66GrowthDepth2SurfaceTest asserts: tables nps_responses + nps_prompt_states + user_tour_states + users.leaderboard_opt_out; classes NpsResponse/NpsResponseController/NpsResponsePolicy/NpsEligibilityService/NpsScoreService, ReferralLeaderboardService/ReferralLeaderboardController/OpsReferralLeaderboardController/LeaderboardOptOutController, UserTourState/TourService/OnboardingTourController, CohortRetentionService/OpsCohortRetentionController; routes (nps.* , growth.leaderboard + opt-out, ops referral-leaderboard, onboarding-tour.* , ops cohort-retention); Vue files (Nps/NpsSurveyModal, Growth/Leaderboard, Tour/TourOverlay + TourTooltip, Ops/Growth/CohortRetention); composables/useOnboardingTour.ts; @floating-ui/vue in package.json; config/nps.php; commands nps:rollup + growth:leaderboard-rollup.

findings.title: Per-category feature tests
findings.evidence: Surface presence is necessary but not sufficient; behavior (gating, anonymisation, server-authoritative tour state, cohort auth) must be asserted.
findings.remediation: Phase66NpsSurveyTest (score validation + category boundaries + double-submit 422 + kill-switch + age gate + dismiss + opt-out + impression). Phase66ReferralLeaderboardTest (ranking + anonymise strips PII + opt-out excluded + self-row de-anonymised + route trust boundaries + isolation). Phase66OnboardingTourTest (advance/complete/dismiss server-authoritative + terminal null payload + milestone-aware skip + backward rejection + isolation). Phase66CohortRetentionServiceTest (super-admin guard + landlord-scoped exclusion + delta math + insufficient_sample). All use CreatesTestData + RefreshDatabase.

findings.title: growth.md runbook extension + alert rows + memory cross-links
findings.evidence: Operators need NPS cadence/kill-switch, leaderboard opt-out/DPA, tour-registry authoring, and cohort-retention docs; the cycle ledger needs memory updates.
findings.remediation: docs/runbooks/growth.md gains NPS + leaderboard + onboarding-tour + cohort-retention sections (config knobs, kill-switch, opt-out/DPA, tour registry authoring guide, cron interaction matrix). alert-thresholds.md rows cross-linked. Memory: project_propmanager_phase66_plan + pending-path + forward-path + MEMORY.md updated at closeout. CodeRabbit code-reviewer run recorded per sub-phase diff.
